# Cost Forecast — 2026-07-06

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-07-01 → 2026-07-05 (non-ACA actuals) + 2026-06-29 → 2026-06-30 (ACA paid rate — see methodology)
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

> **The open question from the 2026-06-29 report is resolved — and the answer changes everything.** Postgres (`psql-town-crier-shared`) was never in billing lag: its Cost Management meters are literally **"B1MS Compute - Free"** (24 hrs/day) and **"Storage Data Stored - Free"** (32 GB) — the Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server free-tier offer (750 B1MS hours + 32 GB storage/month), active on this Pay-As-You-Go subscription. Postgres costs **£0.00/month** for as long as the offer holds, not the £13.50/month rate-card figure every prior report carried. Combined with the ACA monthly free grant (which resets on the 1st and absorbs ~9 days of Container Apps usage each month), the true steady state is **~£11.60/month** — roughly a third of the ~£31/month forecast last week.

## Headline

| Horizon          | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta    | Confidence |
|------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|---------:|------------|
| Rest of month (6–31 Jul) | £11.14     | £72.04         | −£60.90  | Medium-High |
| Next 30 days (6 Jul–4 Aug) | £11.67   | £75.74         | −£64.07  | Medium     |
| Next quarter (6 Jul–4 Oct) | £34.78   | £134.46        | −£99.68  | Medium     |

**TL;DR:** Steady-state is now **~£0.38/day (~£11.60/month)** — down from June's £42.61 because Cosmos is gone, Postgres turned out to be free-tier (£0.00, confirmed at meter level), and the ACA monthly free grant zero-rates the first ~9 days of every month. Azure's forecast is overstated by a factor of **~6.5×** on every horizon: it still carries the phantom £7.43/day plateau artifact (13–22 Jul, ~£50 of imaginary spend) and extrapolates June's migration-inflated blended rate. Full-July projection: **~£11.80** vs June's £42.61.

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **The £7.43/day phantom plateau, 13–22 Jul — ~£50 of the £60.90 rest-of-month over-statement.** Azure projects £5.47–£7.44/day for ten consecutive days starting 13 Jul. This is the same artifact flagged in the 2026-06-27, -28 and -29 reports: the model re-extrapolating the mid-June Cosmos growth ramp. Cosmos (`cosmos-town-crier-shared`) was destroyed 2026-06-27 (commits `21e8fda3`, `26b1604a`); it has had zero cost since 28 Jun and no `Microsoft.DocumentDB` resource exists in the subscription. No current resource can produce £7.43/day.
- **ACA monthly free grant reset — invisible to linear extrapolation.** Container Apps billing stopped dead on 1 Jul (no cost rows at all, 1–6 Jul) because the per-subscription monthly free grant (180,000 vCPU-s / 360,000 GiB-s) reset. June showed the same pattern: ACA billing only *started* on 10 Jun, after ~9 days of grant burn-down. The always-on prod API replica (0.25 vCPU / 0.5 GiB, `minReplicas: 1`) consumes 43,200 GiB-s/day — the grant's binding constraint — so billing resumes ~10 Jul at the post-migration paid rate of ~£0.35/day (Jun 29–30 actuals). Azure's forecast instead spreads a June-average across July, both mis-timing and over-pricing it.
- **June's migration churn baked into the extrapolation.** June contained the Cosmos→Postgres cutover week (26–28 Jun: £0.49–£0.62/day ACA from deployment churn and dual-running), the .NET→Go dual-stack period (1–15 Jun), and Cosmos's final ramp (£1.56/day peak on 25 Jun). None of that recurs. Azure's late-July dailies (~£0.70–£0.98) are roughly double the real post-grant rate (~£0.48/day all-in).
- **Where Azure is accidentally right:** Postgres. Its linear model projects £0 for Postgres because actuals are £0 — the right answer for the wrong reason (it has no idea a free-tier offer is doing the work).

| Azure Database for PostgreSQL | £0.00 | £0.00 | £0.00 | **£0.00/mo — free-tier meters, confirmed** |
| Service Bus | £0.00 | £0.00 | £0.00 | negligible (Basic tier) |
| Storage / Log Analytics / Bandwidth | £0.00 | £0.00 | £0.00 | negligible |

June closed at **£42.61** (of which Cosmos £23.53 in its final month). July is projected at **~£11.80**.

## Recommendations

- **Lower the subscription monthly budget from £50 to £20.**
  **Estimated saving:** £0/month (guard-rail value, zero effort)
  **Bead:** `tc-0qnj` (updated and re-titled this run)
  **Rationale:** The Pulumi-managed `budget-subscription-monthly` (£50, alerts at 50%/100%) was calibrated for the Cosmos-era bill. At the new ~£11.60/month steady state, the 100% alert only fires on a 4.3× anomaly. At £20, the 50% alert (£10) trips near normal month-end and the 100% alert flags genuine runaway spend. One-line Pulumi change.

- **Confirm the Postgres free-tier offer's terms and expiry date.**
  **Estimated saving:** n/a — filed as a question (impact **+£13.50/month** when the offer lapses, likely ~June 2027)
  **Bead:** `tc-jiad9`
  **Rationale:** The entire database tier currently rides on zero-rated free-tier meters (750 B1MS hrs + 32 GB storage/month). Knowing the lapse date — and whether >32 GB storage or a second server breaks eligibility — is needed for FY budgeting and to avoid a surprise +£13.50/month.

**Watch items (no bead — below the £2/month threshold):**

- **ACR storage growth:** 413 MB of 10 GB used, but +330 MB in the last week from CD image pushes. At that rate the included quota exhausts around early 2027; overage is only ~£0.10/GB/month, and Basic ACR does not support retention policies (Premium-only) — the lever would be a scheduled `az acr purge` of untagged manifests via CD. Revisit when usage passes ~5 GB.
- **`job-tc-dev-seed-dev` runs hourly** (cron `0 * * * *`) — consumes free-grant units in dev for <£0.05/month equivalent. Harmless today; worth remembering the cadence is hourly if grant-exhaustion dates start drifting earlier.

**Checked and cleared:** prod `minReplicas: 1` is intentional (cold-start fix, paying customer); dev API scales to zero; Service Bus already Basic; Log Analytics ingestion under free allowance with the two noisy tables already on Basic Logs; no ACA revision sprawl (1 active revision each); no orphaned billable resources. `id-town-crier-cosmos-data` (managed identity, £0) still lingers post-Cosmos — code cleanup only, unchanged from last report.

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals window:** 2026-05-07 → 2026-07-06 (60 days), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` and by `ServiceName`, via Cost Management REST API (`api-version=2023-11-01`). 2026-07-06 is a partial day and was excluded from all run-rates.
- **Split baseline:** non-ACA run-rates use 1–5 Jul actuals (5 clean days). The ACA *paid* rate cannot be observed in July (free grant active), so it uses the 29–30 Jun mean (£0.345/day) — the only two days that are simultaneously post-Cosmos, post-cutover-churn and post-grant-exhaustion. Only 2 days of evidence → the ±£0.70 band on the ACA line; still the honest choice over the churn-inflated 22–28 Jun mean (£0.589) used last week.
- **Grant-exhaustion model:** June's grant lasted through 9 Jun (first billed ACA day: 10 Jun). July's fleet consumes at a similar rate (one always-on 0.25 vCPU/0.5 GiB replica dominates at 43,200 GiB-s/day against the 360,000 GiB-s grant), so billing is modelled resuming 10 Jul, ±2 days.
- **Postgres free tier:** confirmed at meter level (Cost Management query grouped by `Meter`, 26 Jun–5 Jul): "B1MS Compute - Free" 24.0 hrs/day and "Storage Data Stored - Free" ~1.03 GB-months/day (= 32 GB prorated), all at £0.00 on quotaId `PayAsYouGo_2014-09-01`. Forecast assumes the offer holds through the quarter; lapse risk tracked in `tc-jiad9`.
- **Azure forecast:** Cost Management forecast API, 6 Jul – 5 Oct, daily granularity. Horizon windows differ from bottom-up by ≤1 day at the boundary (≤£1 effect) — immaterial against the ~6.5× gap.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** all three stacks (`shared`, `dev`, `prod`) exported and matched to `az resource list` by resource ID. All billable resources accounted for. Azure-side unmatched items are three auto-created, free alerting artifacts (Azure-mobile-app Service Health alert + action group, App Insights Smart Detection action group). Pulumi-side unmatched items are child resources (blob containers, LA tables, PG configs, custom domains, the consumption budget) invisible to `az resource list` — not drift.
- **Exclusions:** 14 Jun spike (£1.26 ACA day — dual .NET+Go deployment churn), 26–28 Jun cutover churn, and all Cosmos history excluded from forward baselines.
